source: Library of Congress, Prints & Photographs Division
license: No known restrictions on publication (public domain — pre-1924 photograph)
title: A "Hanging Valley", Frazer Reach, Alaska
description: 1899 Curtis photograph from the Harriman Alaska Expedition showing a hanging valley at Frazer Reach, Southeast Alaska — the deep-cut glacier-carved terrain that is characteristic of the Chugach region around College Fjord.
alt: Historical 1899 photograph of a hanging valley at Frazer Reach, Alaska, with a snow-capped peak between dark forested ridges and calm reflective water
